document.title: Librarian2 Registry Editor Implementation Architecture
document.purpose: Describe the implementation architecture, modules, and development plan required to build the Librarian2 Registry Editor.
document.author: Lion + ChatGPT

architecture_overview.description: The Librarian2 Registry Editor is implemented as a reducer-driven application that manages registry data and UI state through dispatched actions. The reducer core owns all application state transitions while the UI renders state and dispatches actions.
principles: All application state changes must occur through reducer actions.
UI code must not directly mutate registry data.
Unknown fields must always be preserved.
Form editors operate only when safe; otherwise raw JSON editing is used.

implementation_dependencies.name: lionscliapp
implementation_dependencies.role: Provides command parsing, persistent configuration storage, and command dispatch for the patchboard-run command-line tool.
implementation_dependencies.rationale: Using lionscliapp reduces boilerplate for CLI argument parsing, project configuration management, and command routing, allowing patchboard-run to focus on process lifecycle management rather than CLI infrastructure.
implementation_dependencies.impact_on_architecture: None. lionscliapp is an implementation convenience and is not required by Patchboard, FileTalk, or component specifications.

application_state.description: Central immutable-ish state object managed by the reducer.

actions.description: All operations in the system are represented as dispatched actions.

reducer_modules.description: Reducers handle state transitions for specific areas of responsibility.

behavior: Shows resource IDs sorted by ID
Displays logical type using unicode icons
Does not display title or tags

form_editor_system.description: Structured editing interfaces for common resource types.
form_editor_system.selection_logic: Form editors activate based on type.logical.base.
form_editor_system.safety_rule: Forms must refuse activation if the record contains structures they cannot safely preserve.

record_editor_system.description: The right-hand editor pane edits the JSON record describing a resource entry within the registry. The editor does not modify the underlying resource itself (file, folder, or URL); it modifies the registry record that describes that resource.
record_editor_system.editor_selection_rule: Editors are selected based on the record's type.logical.base and related metadata. If the editor cannot safely handle the record, the Raw Record Editor must be used.

raw_record_editor.description: Fallback editor that displays and edits the raw JSON of the registry record.
raw_record_editor.purpose: Guarantee that any valid registry entry can be edited.

file_record_editor.description: Structured editor for registry records describing files.
file_record_editor.activation_condition: type.logical.base == 'file' and format is not JSON-specific

json_record_editor.description: Specialized editor for registry records describing JSON files.

folder_record_editor.description: Structured editor for registry records describing folders.

url_record_editor.description: Structured editor for registry records describing URLs.

program_record_editor.description: Reserved editor for records describing executable programs.
notes: May expose runtime and invocation metadata from the type.logical.runtime or invoke fields

editor_safety_rules: Editors must update only the fields they explicitly control.
If the record contains structures the editor cannot safely preserve, the Raw Record Editor must be used instead.

json_editor.description: Fallback editor allowing full JSON editing.

script_system.description: User-defined scripts that operate on the selected registry record.
execution_model: resulting record is dispatched through reducer
constraints: scripts must operate only on the selected record

unknown_field_protection.description: The editor must never discard unknown fields.
rules: Reducers must merge updates rather than rebuild entries.
Form editors must update only fields they explicitly control.
Raw JSON editing must always remain available.

phase_2_registry_support.description: Implement loading, saving, and indexing of registry files.

phase_3_ui_framework.description: Establish the main UI layout and navigation behavior.

phase_4_record_editors.description: Implement record editors for the common resource types.

phase_5_automation_and_integration.description: Implement extensibility and external tool integration.
tasks: Implement script registration and hotkey system

80_20_guideline.description: Focus development effort on the most common operations.
